name: B&F Finance (Texas Warrant)
description_short: B&F Finance is a Texas-based personal installment lender founded in 1973, with 25+ branches across Texas and Oklahoma offering fixed-payment loans funded to your debit card.
description_long:
B&F Finance Corporation was founded in 1973 with a single loan office in Texas and has grown over more than 50 years into a network of over 25 branch locations across Texas and Oklahoma. The company is family-led and headquartered in the Dallas–Fort Worth area, with expansion into San Antonio, the Rio Grande Valley, and Oklahoma occurring through the 1980s and 1990s. It operates under various local trade names including B&R Finance and Texas Finance Company, with customer relations managed through Victory Management Services in Lake Dallas, TX.

The company offers personal installment loans with fixed payments, designed to help borrowers handle unexpected or everyday expenses without resorting to payday loans. Loans can be used for debt consolidation, auto repairs, home improvements, medical bills, emergency expenses, and moving costs. Borrowers can apply online or in person at a local branch, receive a quick credit decision, and get funds deposited directly to a debit card upon approval. A mobile app for account management is listed as coming soon.

What distinguishes B&F Finance is its community-oriented, locally staffed branch model rather than a purely digital approach. Each location is described as operating independently and in compliance with state regulations, with staff who understand the local community. The management team collectively holds over 100 years of experience in consumer finance, and the company emphasizes straightforward loan terms, honest communication, and treating customers like neighbors — a consistent message throughout their public materials.

Honestly, B&F Finance presents itself as a responsible alternative to payday lenders, and its 50-year track record and local branch presence are genuine strengths for borrowers who value in-person service. However, the website discloses no APR ranges, loan amounts, fees, or repayment term details — critical information any informed borrower needs before applying. Prospective customers should plan to call or visit a branch to get actual cost details and compare them against other lenders before committing.
address: 1717 S Pleasant Valley Rd, Austin, TX 78741

company_info.headquarters: 1717 S Pleasant Valley Rd, Austin, TX 78741

pros: Over 50 years in operation — founded in 1973 with a documented expansion history
25+ physical branch locations across Texas and Oklahoma for in-person service
Explicitly positioned as an alternative to payday loans with fixed installment payments
Fast debit card funding upon approval — no waiting for a check or ACH transfer
Apply online or walk into a local branch — flexible application options
Management team with over 100 combined years in consumer finance
Bilingual service offered — Spanish-language site available
cons: No APR, interest rates, fee schedules, or loan amount ranges disclosed on the website
Only available in Texas and Oklahoma — no service for out-of-state borrowers
Mobile app is listed as 'Coming Soon' and not yet available to customers
Customer relations are handled by a separate entity (Victory Management Services), which may complicate dispute resolution
No online pre-qualification or rate check tool visible on the site
best_for: Texas or Oklahoma residents who prefer working with a local branch and in-person loan officer
Borrowers looking for a structured installment loan to replace or avoid high-cost payday loan cycles
People covering unexpected expenses — medical bills, car repairs, or emergency costs — who need fast debit card funding
Consumers who value a long-established, community-rooted lender over a fintech startup
diagnosis: B&F Finance is best suited for Texas and Oklahoma residents who want a face-to-face lending relationship and the stability of a 50-year-old installment lender — particularly those trying to avoid the payday loan cycle. The main caveat is that the website provides zero transparency on pricing: no APR ranges, no loan amounts, and no fee disclosures, so borrowers must contact a branch to learn the actual cost of borrowing before they can make an informed comparison.
services: Personal installment loans with fixed payments
In-person application at local branch offices
Local branch network across Texas and Oklahoma
